steps.summary:
* creating README.md
* creating .formatter.exs

* creating test
* creating test/test_helper.exs
* creating test/word_count_test.exs

Your Mix project was created successfully.
You can use "mix" to compile it, test it, and more:

    cd word_count
    mix test

Run "mix help" for more commands.

file	.formatter.exs
file	.gitignore
file	README.md
dir	lib
file	mix.exs
dir	test

defmodule WordCountTest do
  use ExUnit.Case
  doctest WordCount

  test "greets the world" do
    assert WordCount.hello() == :world
  end
end

defmodule WordCount do
  @moduledoc """
  Documentation for `WordCount`.
  """

  @doc """
  Hello world.

  ## Examples

      iex> WordCount.hello()
      :world

  """
  def hello do
    :world
  end
end

defmodule WordCount.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
    [
      app: :word_count,
      version: "0.1.0",
      elixir: "~> 1.15",
      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: deps()
    ]
  end

  # Run "mix help compile.app" to learn about applications.
  def application do
    [
      extra_applications: [:logger]
    ]
  end

  # Run "mix help deps" to learn about dependencies.
  defp deps do
    [
      # {:dep_from_hexpm, "~> 0.3.0"},
      # {:dep_from_git, git: "https://github.com/elixir-lang/my_dep.git", tag: "0.1.0"}
    ]
  end
end

args.new_string:
# Run "mix help deps" to learn about dependencies.
  defp deps do
    [
      # {:dep_from_hexpm, "~> 0.3.0"},
      # {:dep_from_git, git: "https://github.com/elixir-lang/my_dep.git", tag: "0.1.0"}
    ]
  end

  defmodule Escript do
    def project do
      [
        escript: escript()
      ]
    end

    defp escript do
      [main_module: WordCount.CLI]
    end
  end
end
